fields.name: Influenza
fields.description: Acute viral respiratory infection caused by influenza viruses.
fields.treatment: Rest, fluids, antipyretics; antivirals in high-risk groups.

fields.name: Tuberculosis
fields.description: Chronic bacterial infection by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, usually pulmonary.
fields.treatment: Multi-drug regimen (rifampicin, isoniazid and others) for 6 months.

fields.name: Malaria
fields.description: Mosquito-borne Plasmodium infection; falciparum can be fatal.
fields.treatment: Artemisinin-based combination therapy; supportive care.

fields.name: Type 2 Diabetes
fields.description: Insulin resistance causing chronic hyperglycaemia.
fields.treatment: Lifestyle change, metformin, escalating therapy; glucose monitoring.

fields.name: Hypertension
fields.description: Persistently elevated arterial blood pressure.
fields.treatment: Lifestyle change plus ACE inhibitors, ARBs, CCBs or diuretics.

fields.name: Asthma
fields.description: Chronic airway inflammation with reversible obstruction.
fields.treatment: Inhaled bronchodilators and corticosteroids; avoid triggers.

fields.name: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease
fields.description: Progressive airflow limitation, usually from smoking.
fields.treatment: Bronchodilators, inhaled steroids, smoking cessation, oxygen.

fields.name: Pneumonia
fields.description: Acute infection of the lung parenchyma.
fields.treatment: Antibiotics guided by severity; oxygen and fluids as needed.

fields.name: Ischaemic Heart Disease
fields.description: Reduced coronary blood flow causing angina or infarction.
fields.treatment: Antiplatelets, statins, beta-blockers; revascularisation if indicated.

fields.name: Ischaemic Stroke
fields.description: Sudden neurological deficit from cerebral arterial occlusion.
fields.treatment: Acute reperfusion where eligible; antiplatelets, risk-factor control.

fields.name: HIV Infection
fields.description: Human immunodeficiency virus causing progressive immune failure.
fields.treatment: Lifelong combination antiretroviral therapy; opportunistic-infection care.

fields.name: Hepatitis B
fields.description: Viral infection of the liver by hepatitis B virus.
fields.treatment: Antivirals in chronic disease; vaccination prevents infection.

fields.name: Cholera
fields.description: Acute watery diarrhoea from Vibrio cholerae; epidemic-prone.
fields.treatment: Aggressive oral/IV rehydration; antibiotics in severe cases.

fields.name: Typhoid Fever
fields.description: Systemic infection by Salmonella Typhi.
fields.treatment: Appropriate antibiotics; fluids and supportive care.

fields.name: Measles
fields.description: Highly contagious viral illness with rash; vaccine-preventable.
fields.treatment: Supportive care, vitamin A; manage complications.

fields.name: COVID-19
fields.description: Respiratory illness caused by SARS-CoV-2.
fields.treatment: Supportive care; oxygen and specific therapies in severe disease.

fields.name: Gastroenteritis
fields.description: Inflammation of the GI tract, usually infectious.
fields.treatment: Oral rehydration and supportive care; antibiotics rarely needed.

fields.name: Urinary Tract Infection
fields.description: Bacterial infection of the urinary tract.
fields.treatment: Short-course antibiotics; increase fluids.

fields.name: Iron-Deficiency Anaemia
fields.description: Reduced haemoglobin from inadequate iron stores.
fields.treatment: Oral iron and treat the underlying cause.

fields.name: Major Depressive Disorder
fields.description: Persistent low mood and loss of interest impairing function.
fields.treatment: Psychotherapy and/or antidepressants.

fields.name: Migraine
fields.description: Recurrent moderate-to-severe headache, often with aura.
fields.treatment: Acute analgesia/triptans; prophylaxis if frequent.

fields.name: Osteoarthritis
fields.description: Degenerative joint disease with cartilage loss.
fields.treatment: Exercise, weight loss, analgesia; joint replacement if severe.

fields.name: Chronic Kidney Disease
fields.description: Progressive, irreversible loss of kidney function.
fields.treatment: Control BP and diabetes; manage complications; dialysis if end-stage.

fields.name: Hypothyroidism
fields.description: Underactive thyroid causing metabolic slowing.
fields.treatment: Levothyroxine replacement, dose-titrated.

fields.name: Epilepsy
fields.description: Recurrent unprovoked seizures from abnormal brain activity.
fields.treatment: Antiepileptic drugs; treat the underlying cause where present.

fields.name: Dengue Fever
fields.description: Mosquito-borne viral febrile illness; can progress to severe disease.
fields.treatment: Supportive care and careful fluid management; avoid NSAIDs.

fields.name: Bacterial Meningitis
fields.description: Acute infection of the meninges; a medical emergency.
fields.treatment: Urgent empirical IV antibiotics; supportive care.

fields.name: Peptic Ulcer Disease
fields.description: Mucosal ulceration of stomach or duodenum.
fields.treatment: PPIs, H. pylori eradication; stop NSAIDs.

fields.name: Allergic Rhinitis
fields.description: IgE-mediated nasal inflammation from allergen exposure.
fields.treatment: Allergen avoidance, antihistamines, intranasal steroids.

fields.dosage: 500 mg day 1, then 250 mg daily x4

fields.indications: UTI, GI and systemic gram-negative infections.

fields.description: Additive bleeding risk.
fields.recommendation: Avoid unless specifically indicated; monitor closely.

fields.description: NSAIDs raise bleeding risk with anticoagulants.
Ciprofloxacin potentiates warfarin, raising INR.
Metronidazole inhibits warfarin metabolism.
Rifampicin induces metabolism, reducing warfarin effect.
fields.recommendation: Expect higher dose need; monitor INR.

fields.description: Ibuprofen can block aspirin's antiplatelet effect.
fields.recommendation: Separate dosing; review need for both.

fields.description: Additive GI ulceration and bleeding risk.
NSAIDs blunt ACE-inhibitor effect and risk renal impairment.
NSAIDs reduce diuretic efficacy and risk renal injury.
fields.recommendation: Monitor fluid status and renal function.

fields.description: Dual RAAS blockade risks hyperkalaemia and renal failure.
Risk of first-dose hypotension.
Additive serotonergic effect and raised TCA levels.
fields.recommendation: Avoid or reduce TCA dose; watch for toxicity.

fields.description: Increased statin exposure and myopathy risk.
Iron chelates ciprofloxacin, reducing absorption.
fields.recommendation: Separate doses by at least 2 hours.

fields.description: Corticosteroids raise blood glucose, opposing hypoglycaemics.

fields.name: Chest X-ray
fields.description: Plain radiograph of the chest.

fields.name: Electrocardiogram
fields.description: Records the heart's electrical activity.

fields.name: Upper GI Endoscopy
fields.description: Direct visualisation of upper GI tract.

fields.name: Lumbar Puncture
fields.description: CSF sampling from the lumbar spine.

fields.name: Spirometry
fields.description: Measures lung volumes and airflow.
fields.indications: Diagnose and grade asthma and COPD.

fields.name: Full Blood Count
fields.purpose: Screen for anaemia, infection and clotting cells.

fields.title: Understanding Hypertension
fields.summary: Why blood pressure matters and how it is managed.
fields.body: Most hypertension is symptomless; regular measurement and adherence to medication and lifestyle change prevent stroke, heart and kidney disease.

fields.title: Living with Type 2 Diabetes
fields.body: Consistent glucose control through diet, activity and medication prevents long-term complications affecting eyes, nerves and kidneys.

fields.title: Recognising an Asthma Attack
fields.body: Increasing breathlessness, wheeze and reliever use signal worsening asthma; seek urgent help when a reliever no longer controls symptoms.

fields.title: Preventing Malaria
fields.summary: Bite avoidance, prophylaxis and early treatment.
fields.body: Insecticide-treated nets, indoor spraying and prompt testing of any fever in endemic areas reduce malaria deaths.

fields.title: Completing TB Treatment
fields.body: Tuberculosis needs months of combination therapy; stopping early drives drug resistance and relapse. Directly observed therapy improves cure rates.
